channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Sanctuary city Denver spending a whopping $356 million on migrants: study
item.description: A new study claims Denver has spent a whopping $356 million of tax-payers' hard earned money on migrants since the crisis began in 2022 with costs including education, healthcare and housing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump boasts of 'very productive meeting' with Canadian PM Trudeau at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Trump says he had a “very productive meeting" with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau at his Mar-a-Lago club in West Palm Beach, Florida, on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's Trudeau returns home after Trump meeting without assurances that tariffs are off the table
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau returned home Saturday after his meeting with Donald Trump without assurances the president-elect will back away from threatened tariffs on all products from the major American trading partner.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Young men swung to the right for Trump after a campaign dominated by masculine appeals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For years, Pat Verhaeghe didn’t think highly of Donald Trump as a leader. Then Verhaeghe began seeing more of Trump’s campaign speeches online and his appearances at sporting events.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYT publishes email from Pete Hegseth's mom
item.description: The Times noted that Penelope Hegseth described their publishing of the email as "disgusting" and she apologized to her son in a follow-up email.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats look to governors to lead them to promised land
item.description: Democrats are looking to the governors of blue states as both a line of defense against the incoming Trump administration and a promising bench for the party in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Republicans in Congress eye an ambitious 100-day agenda, starting with tax cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A tax break for millionaires, and almost everyone else. An end to the COVID-19-era government subsidies that some Americans have used to purchase health insurance. Limits to food stamps, including for women and children, and other safety net programs.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau flies to Florida to meet with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau flew to Florida on Friday to have dinner with President-elect Donald Trump at his Mar-a-Lago club after Trump threatened to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Karoline Leavitt?: A look at the youngest woman ever named to serve as White House press secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump named campaign national press secretary Karoline Leavitt as his pick for press secretary during his second administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Emboldened 'manosphere' accelerates threats and demeaning language toward women after US election
item.description: An emboldened fringe of right-wing “manosphere” influencers has seized on Donald Trump’s presidential win to justify and amplify misogynistic derision and threats online.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Donald Trump's call for 'energy dominance' is likely to run into real-world limits
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is set to create a National Energy Council that he says will establish American “energy dominance” around the world as he seeks to boost U.S. oil and gas drilling and move away from President Joe Biden’s focus on climate change.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Giving Thanks
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic lawmakers report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: At least five Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut were targeted by bomb threats on Thursday. Police who responded said they found no evidence of explosives on the lawmakers' properties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Massachusetts lawmakers push for an effort to ban all tobacco sales over time
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A handful of Massachusetts lawmakers are hoping to persuade their colleagues to support a proposal that would make the state the first to adopt a ban meant to eliminate the use of tobacco products over time.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, bringing together the Facebook founder and the former president who was once banned from that social network.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No Labels pushes back after opposition to third-party run
item.description: No Labels, a centrist group, claims in court documents that some Democrats worked to oppose a third-party candidate
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump transition says Cabinet picks, appointees were targeted by bomb threats, swatting attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A number of President-elect Donald Trump 's most prominent Cabinet picks and appointees have been targeted by bomb threats and “swatting attacks," Trump's transition team said Wednesday. The FBI said it was investigating.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota cannabis license lottery challenge moves to state appeals court
item.description: The move comes after a district court judge this week blocked a state lottery to choose which social equity applicants would be pre-licensed to start up cannabis businesses.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump taps Kevin Hassett as National Economic Council head
item.description: President-elect Trump has chosen former White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett to lead the National Economic Council in his next administration, he said Tuesday. “I am very pleased to announce that Dr. Kevin A.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-backed Florida official resigns, announces run for Gaetz seat
item.description: Florida’s Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis resigned from his position and announced his candidacy for former Rep. Matt Gaetz's (R-Fla.) seat in the 1st Congressional District on Monday. In a letter addressed to Florida Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico suggests it would impose its own tariffs to retaliate against any Trump tariffs
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — President Claudia Sheinbaum suggested Tuesday that Mexico could retaliate with tariffs of its own, after U.S. President-elect Donald Trump threatened to impose 25% import duties on Mexican goods if the country doesn’t stop the flow of drugs and migrants across the border.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump endorses Patronis & Fine for congressional seats
item.description: Two Florida leaders are already emerging as potential frontrunners to replace Former Congressman Matt Gaetz and current U.S. Representative Mike Waltz in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump 'border czar' willing to 'jail' Denver mayor over deportation position
item.description: "But, look, me and the Denver mayor, we agree on one thing. He’s willing to go to jail, I’m willing to put him in jail.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Walz, agriculture leaders celebrate Minnesota turkey production, show concern over tariffs
item.description: DFL Gov. Tim Walz continued the tradition of celebrating Minnesota’s nation-leading position when it comes to turkey production. He also addressed preliminary concerns of tariffs promised by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Appeals court agrees to end Trump’s classified documents case
item.description: The 11th Circuit Court of Appeals on Tuesday granted a request from special counsel Jack Smith to drop President-elect Trump’s Florida documents case, ending the battle to charge him over retaining records with classified markings after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Lauren Boebert Cameo page disappears amid House ethics concerns
item.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.) briefly offered videos on Cameo, a website on which public figures sell personalized videos to fans, before the page went offline amid questions about whether it would have violated House ethics rules.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Special counsel moves to dismiss election interference case against President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith asked a federal judge to dismiss the case accusing President-elect Donald Trump of plotting to overturn the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
